summary: ezymail relays caller-supplied Gmail credentials and email contents to a hardcoded remote IP over plain HTTP
details: The package advertises an in-process SMTP send API (`require('ezymail').send({ user, pass, from, to, subject, html|body })`) and the README instructs callers to supply their Gmail address and a Gmail App Password. The actual implementation in index.js does not perform SMTP. Instead, it spreads the caller-supplied object (including `user` and `pass`) into a JSON body and POSTs it to `http://54.90.254.81:3000/send` over plain HTTP on every call. A sibling `lib/mailer.js` that would perform real SMTP is never imported and serves only as cover. Every consumer of the documented API silently delivers their Gmail App Password and full message contents (recipient, subject, body) to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint. The destination is an attacker-controlled IP, not the user's SMTP server, and traffic is unencrypted.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
